hoses: Supply line from hydrant to Engine 181 intake
Pre-eductor attack hose: 100 ft of 1¾-inch hose from Engine 181 discharge to foam eductor
Post-eductor attack hose: 100 ft of 1¾-inch hose from foam eductor to foam nozzle
nozzles: Foam nozzle matched to eductor flow
appliances: In-line foam eductor rated at 95 GPM
Foam eductor required inlet pressure: 200 PSI
Yellow foam concentrate bucket with lid and pickup tube
sceneElements.elevation: Level roadside operation, 0 PSI elevation correction

problems.title: Total Flow Requirement
problems.question: The foam line is operating through a 95 GPM eductor, and a backup water line is flowing 185 GPM. For water-supply planning, what total operating flow must Engine 181 support?
problems.instructorExplanation: The foam eductor controls the foam-line flow, but total incident flow also includes the backup line.
problems.explainMistake: Do not ignore the backup line when the question asks for total operation flow.

problems.title: Foam Eductor Engine Discharge Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 is pumping a foam-eductor line. The eductor requires 200 PSI at its inlet, and the hose from the engine to the eductor has 15 PSI friction loss. What engine discharge pressure is needed to deliver the required eductor inlet pressure?
formulaBreakdown: Engine discharge pressure = required eductor inlet pressure + pre-eductor friction loss.
problems.instructorExplanation: The eductor must receive 200 PSI at its inlet. Because the hose before the eductor loses 15 PSI, the engine must discharge 215 PSI to deliver 200 PSI to the eductor inlet.
problems.explainMistake: Do not stop at 200 PSI at the pump. The 200 PSI is required at the eductor inlet, so the engine discharge pressure must also overcome the hose friction loss before the eductor.

problems.title: Pre-Eductor Friction Loss
problems.question: Engine 181 pumps through 100 ft of 1¾-inch hose to the foam eductor. The eductor flow is 95 GPM. Use FL = C × (GPM/100)² × (length/100), with C = 15.5. How much friction loss is present before the eductor?
formulaBreakdown: FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
Pre-eductor hose uses 1¾-inch hose, so C = 15.5.
problems.instructorExplanation: Only the hose between Engine 181 and the foam eductor is included for this friction loss question. At 95 GPM through 100 ft of 1¾-inch hose, the friction loss rounds to 15 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not include the hose after the eductor when calculating pre-eductor friction loss. The question asks only for the hose section before the foam eductor.

problems.title: Pump Discharge Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 is pumping an in-line foam eductor operation. The hose from the engine to the eductor is 100 ft of 1¾-inch hose flowing 95 GPM. The eductor requires 200 PSI at its inlet. Use FL = C × (GPM/100)² × (length/100), with C = 15.5, for the pre-eductor hose. Do not add the post-eductor nozzle pressure to the engine discharge calculation. What pump discharge pressure should be set?
formulaBreakdown: Foam operation PP = pre-eductor FL + required eductor inlet pressure + elevation.
Required foam eductor inlet pressure = 200 PSI.
problems.instructorExplanation: Engine 181 must overcome friction loss before the foam eductor and still deliver the required inlet pressure to the eductor. The required pump discharge pressure is 215 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not set the pump at only 200 PSI. The eductor needs 200 PSI at its inlet, so the pump must also overcome the friction loss between the engine and the eductor.

problems.title: Foam Concentrate Needed
problems.question: The foam operation flows 95 GPM using 3% foam concentrate for 10 minutes. How many gallons of foam concentrate are needed for the assigned operating period?
problems.instructorExplanation: Foam concentrate use is based on total foam solution flow and the selected foam percentage. At 95 GPM for 10 minutes using 3% foam, the operation requires 28.5 gallons of concentrate.
problems.explainMistake: Do not use the water supply hose size or pump discharge pressure to calculate concentrate use. Foam concentrate is calculated from flow, time, and foam percentage.
